question.text: Which of the following statements about promoters of a company is correct?

answer.text: Promoters are institutional investors who invest after the IPO
Promoters have the lowest risk exposure among all investor categories
Promoters cannot exercise voting rights in the company
Promoters provide the initial capital and bear the highest risk

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Promoters are the founders who provide the initial capital to set up the company. They bear the highest risk because they invest at the earliest stage when the business model is unproven. They also typically exercise control through significant shareholding. **The Trap:** Option B is the opposite of reality — promoters have the highest risk, not the lowest, as they invest when the company has no track record. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) covers promoter obligations in IPOs, and Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) tests promoter classification. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When analysing a company, always check promoter holding percentage — a declining promoter stake can signal lack of confidence in the business.
